title: The Sparkling Dust of Book World
summary: Hattie the Mouse and Dimitar seek the wizard Seeker's help to resolve a heated argument over Dimitar’s collection of dragonet books. Dimitar is protective of his library because a friend once lost a precious book in the ocean, turning it into mush. With a compromise reached and a donation of extra books from Isabella, the trio tests a new potion made from magical book dust. They are transported to Book World, a realm where sentient volumes waddle on tiny feet and clouds are made of pages. After meeting a fact-filled Penguin Book and a sturdy Greenland Book, they return home with a superhero tale about Kitty and her cat-like powers.

highlightQuote.text: "Are you a peng... are you a penguin made out of books?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who is it? says Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie... and Dimitar. We were having a little bit of an argument yesterday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Another argument? Well, come right in and you tell me all about it, says Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But, what kind of argument... we didn't know if they were... we didn't know which... which... which... um...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of argument?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Which... which... which book I could have, says Hattie. And Dimitar, we were having an argument about which books we could have.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dimitar said, "You can't have any of them, Hattie!" And I said, "Yes I can!" And then we got into an argument.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Um, Dimitar wanted all the books for himself? says Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Oh, I see. And then we got into a big argument.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and did you call each other names or say mean things to each other a little bit?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Said mean things and called ourselves names.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Did anybody bite anybody else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No no, I just made a big, big, big, big, big, big, big hole in one of Dimitar's fishes because we were trying to share a fish while we were picking out the books. While we were having an argument.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And Dimitar, how did you feel about that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sad. Dimitar was the one who was having the fish, and I took a big bite and ate all of the body and the tail.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Dimitar only had the head left to eat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And was he mad about that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. And then we tried to pick out which books we want to share and read, and then we got into the argument.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar, why did you feel like you only needed to have all the books and Hattie couldn't have any?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I liked every one of... every one was about dragonets. And Hattie wanted all of them and I didn't, and I want all of them for myself.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ugh. Well, Seeker says that's really hard, and I know what it's like to really want to have all the books because I love books myself. You can see here in my castle I've got a big library of lots and lots of very interesting books about history and about magic and about the other worlds that we go to with our potions, and about science and about fossils and all kinds of things. And I want to have them all for myself, so I can understand that. But you two are married, and so it's very important for you to learn how to share things, Dimitar and Hattie. So what can we do about this?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, Hattie, do you have any suggestions for a way that the two of you could feel better about everything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know! says Hattie. Me and her, Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, says Seeker, you both have things that you need, and maybe if you tell each other what you need, then you could find a way for you both to get what you need.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need books, says Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, you need books. And what does Dimitar say to Hattie about his books?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, well, I just want to make sure that nobody takes my books and loses them because that happened to me once. I lent one of my dragonet books to my dragonet friend and he was trying to read it while he was flying, and he was flying over the ocean and he dropped it in the ocean! And it was horrible and I, you know, I couldn't... I even dove down and found the book but it was all wet and then it all just turned to mush. So I just don't want anybody to take my books and lose them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm not losing anything, so you can borrow some of my books. There's a pile of them in the basement that you can take one of them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi mouse, does you want to borrow some of them? Because that bin has a lot of them for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that was Isabella talking about your books?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie says, "Oh, thank you!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just need to go down, just need to go through the kitchen, turn a door open, go down some stairs, and then you're there. And go down there and get a bin with all kind of all sorts of books I don't want to read anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie says, "Wow, that sounds amazing, thank you. But I think once Dimitar and I go back home to Dimitar's castle, we're still going to have to figure out how to share the books there, even if I have some of my own." And Dimitar says, "Yeah, I guess so, because I don't want you to lose my books." And Hattie says, "Well, maybe I could promise to use your books in a way where you won't be worried that I'll lose them." And Seeker says, "Oh, how could we do that, do you think?" And Hattie says, "Well, maybe I could just promise um, not to leave the castle with any of your dragonet books, so then they won't end up in the water or anywhere else." And what did Dimitar say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, what if I promise not even to take your books out of your library, and I just read them while I'm sitting there? I don't try to walk off with them like I did yesterday, said Hattie. Would that be okay?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Yeah, that would be okay." And Seeker says, "There, you see! Everybody got what they needed. And also our friend Isabella offered even more books."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, maybe to celebrate we should test this new potion that I just made, said Seeker. It contains quite a few ingredients including maple syrup and feathers and water, but also there was a book that was very old and it had gotten so um, dried up and moldy that I couldn't read any of the words, and so I just ground it up into powder and mixed it into the potion. And I think this potion might take us to a very interesting place. Would you like to try it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would! And what about that wet book?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The wet book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I could go down and get it, but I need to put on special gloves if my mom has any of them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. And Dimitar said, "Well that wet book really got destroyed, it just turned into mush. We can't turn it back into a book."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no, no, we're going to grind it up into powder.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Have... so...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dimitar said, "Well, okay, I'll go back and get it." Boop boop boop. They came back. Boop boop boop. "Here it is!" And it was in a baggy and it just looked like a ball of yuck.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker, can you grind that up and make... and do you want to add it to all of your potions?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Okay, I'll give it a try." He ground it up and dried it and stirred it and mushed it around and sifted it and filtered it, and then at the end, he had some very interesting sparkly book dust. And he added that to the potion as the final ingredient. And he stirred it up and he said, "Okay, everybody take a sip." Shloop shloop shloop. And so the three of them took a sip. And what was the rhyme this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then with a whoosh, they all reappeared...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was only one time!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, sorry. Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime. And then again, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime, Sub-Ime. And then with a whoosh, they appeared in the World of Books where everything was made of paper and pages and covers and bindings and stacks of books and piles of books and towers of books and books everywhere, gigantic books and tiny books. And there were clouds drifting through the sky made of fluffy little books.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And were there book people?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they didn't see any at first. And Seeker said, "This is amazing, I wonder what all these books are about."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie said, "I wonder if there's any book people."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Book people, like what?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Um, excuse me, what's that over there?" And he pointed to something that was moving towards them. And what was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was, it looked like a book but it had two little feet sticking out the bottom and it was waddling toward them. Waddle waddle waddle waddle waddle waddle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Are you a peng... are you a penguin made out of books?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Um, no, I'm just a book about penguins. Um, but I would be happy to tell you um, penguin facts if you're interested because I know a lot of them because I'm a book about penguins as you can see. Yep. Um, welcome to Book World. And um, what do you want to know about penguins?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Ah... about if... about...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Which snowy continent they live in. If the book says about that?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It does! Um, penguins live in a lot of places around the world including Antarctica, South America, and Africa."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Are those snowy places?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Well, parts of them are snowy. What else?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Did they only live in Greenland, which is very icy and snowy?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "My goodness," said the penguin book. "That's a very interesting and insightful question. I'll have to check." Read read read read read read read. And the penguin book did a very interesting thing which is that it read itself for a few minutes to try to figure out the answer. And it said, "Hmm, there's no mention of Greenland in me, so I can't say for sure, but I don't think they live there."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "I see a book all about Greenland walking towards us!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there it was. Thump thump thump thump. Because it had big icy boots. And it said, "Uh, did somebody ask a question about Greenland?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Yes, do penguins live in Greenland?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "What are penguins?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Penguin book, can you tell the Greenland book about penguins from your book?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, yeah. Um, penguins are a type of flightless bird that lives in cold places and um, dives in the water to catch fish." And the Greenland book said, "Oh, no, none of those." And Seeker said, "Well this is very interesting, and I love reading books and talking to them is um, pretty amazing. But um, I think we'd better head home. Do you want to talk to one more book?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Yeah, if there's one more about cats or fish or mice or prehistoric animals."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, well," said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "My favorite ones."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Those, wow, those are wonderful topics, and this world of books, I'm sure it has books about all of those things. Let's look around and see what we can find. Oh, here's one," said Seeker. "Hmm, it's just a book, it's not a book person." And he picked it up, and it was "Kitty and Her Cat Crew Visit Isabella's House." "Huh, have you read this one, Isabella?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Huh."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It's about a person named Kitty and she has cat-like superpowers."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Oh, she's... she's one of my superhero friends."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh! And well this is a story about how she comes to visit you at your house on Cornell Street. That must have been a lot of fun. You want to read..."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "You want to read... you want to read it to me?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "I would love to read it to you. You know what we could do? I think we can bring this book back with us from Book World to my castle, and then we can read it when we get back home."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "We need to ask the book people. Book people! Where are you? Book people!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, hello," said the Greenland book. "Yes," said the penguin book.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Um, can we take one of your books home?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Ah, is it a book person or just a book?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "It's just a book."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh yeah, you can take that home with you as long as you bring it back."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "We will!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Okay. Enjoy!" And so they took the "Going Home Potion" and went back to Seeker's castle and they read the book. But the story of that book is a story for another time. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A spider! Shadow puppet!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Shadow puppet time. Okay. Oh, a spider shadow. I like it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Doing the llama?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Llama shadow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And now I'm going to do my bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The bird shadow. Very nice. All right, now that we're done with Stars and Stories, it's time to get tucked in. So, let's turn out...

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Kitty
characters.description: Isabella’s loyal companion and a valiant superhero, this feline-costumed commander harnesses agile, cat-like powers to lead an intrepid crew through the drifting wonders of Balloon World. Bound by a mission of guardianship, she navigates the whimsical floating realm on daring adventures, rescuing lost treasures and protecting every creature she encounters.

characters.name: Penguin Book
characters.description: A sentient book in Book World that waddles on tiny feet and shares facts about penguins.

characters.name: Greenland Book
characters.description: A sturdy sentient book from Book World that wears icy boots.

places.name: Seeker's Castle
places.description: Behind an unassuming facade and a solitary doorbell lies Seeker’s home, a sprawling magical fortress that serves as a vital sanctuary for weary legends. The interior expands into a labyrinthine refuge where an expansive library of mystical books towers beside a potion closet that acts as the true gateway to adventure. This fortress is a hub for calls to action and well-earned rest, where Wobbly Whiskers waits to greet the Seeker and Buddy the Mouse—though the warmth of their homecoming is inevitably punctuated by his latest sticky mess upon the rug.

places.name: Isabella's House
places.description: A home on Cornell Street with a basement containing bins of storybooks.

places.name: Dimitar's Castle
places.description: Perched upon the scorched crags of the volcano, the weathered stone fortress where Dimitar and Hattie reside is dominated by a massive bell tower that echoes through the rising sulfurous mists.

places.name: Book World
places.description: A magical dimension where everything, from the inhabitants to the clouds, is made of paper and bindings.
